business.headline: Prolonged Gulf conflict could be catastrophic for oil markets, says Aramco chiefSpeaking during a media call after the company released its 2025 financial results, Saudi Aramco CEO Amin Nasser said the disruption represents the most serious crisis faced by the region's oil and gas sector in recent years.

EPF rate stays at 8.25%: Should investors continue with VPF or choose SIPs?For many salaried employees, EPF and Voluntary Provident Fund (VPF) are trusted tools for retirement savings. But with the EPF rate staying at 8.25%, some investors are wondering if they should keep contributing to VPF or move extra savings into SIPs for better long-term returns?

Live: Government invokes Essential Commodities Act amid shortage across IndiaLPG Shortage Live Updates: A growing LPG cylinder shortage is disrupting restaurants and hotels across several Indian cities, raising concerns over a widening gas supply crisis. Hospitality associations have warned that eateries are struggling to procure commercial LPG cylinders, forcing some establishments to shut operations temporarily. The shortage is being reported in cities such as Mumbai, Bengaluru and Kolkata, where restaurant bodies say supplies have become irregular over the past few days. The government has asked oil companies and refineries to increase LPG production to stabilise supply. Track this live blog for the latest updates on the LPG cylinder shortage, city-wise impact on restaurants, supply disruptions, and steps being taken to address the crisis.

When crude oil hit a record $147 per barrel in 2008 and the reason was not warCrude oil briefly touched $120 amid Gulf tensions, reviving memories of the historic 2008 oil shock when prices hit $147. But unlike this conflict, the 2008 surge was not driven by war or geopolitics. So what pushed oil prices so high? Read on to find out.

Why have crude oil prices fallen below $100, and will it go up again?Crude oil prices had surged above $100 per barrel on Monday but fell today after recent comments from US President Donald Trump. Experts say that even though prices have fallen today, the risk to global oil supply has not gone away.

LPG shortage shuts 20% of Mumbai hotels and restaurants Live UpdatesThe association warned that up to 50% of hotels in the city could shut down within the next two days if the gas supply situation does not improve.
Sensex jumps 500 points, Nifty above 24,100; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex added 379.79 points to 77,945.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 106.40 points to 24,134.45 as of 9:28 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash again or open higher today?Markets have been under pressure in recent days due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Last week, both benchmark indices, the Sensex and the Nifty, recorded their worst weekly performance in more than a year.

Why oil controls the world economyOil has shaped wars, alliances and global economies for more than a century. A sudden disruption in supply can shake financial markets, push up inflation and send governments scrambling for solutions.

Trump sons-backed Aureus to merge with drone firm Powerus targeting Pentagon salesDrones have become a major procurement priority for the Pentagon and are widely used in Ukraine, where dense air defense systems near the front lines limit the deployment of conventional aircraft.

Stock markets sink worldwide as oil prices briefly spike close to $120 per barrelThe S&P 500 fell 1.3 per cent, coming off its worst week since October. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 721 points, or 1.5 per cent, as of 9:35 am Eastern time, and the Nasdaq composite was 1.2 per cent lower. That followed even worse losses in European and Asian stock markets.

G7 nations hold off on emergency oil stock release as Gulf crisis lifts pricesThe bloc of the world's seven most powerful economies said it is prepared to act to stabilise oil markets if needed but believes the situation has not yet reached a stage requiring coordinated measures such as a joint release of strategic petroleum stocks.

Keep your PPF, SSY, NPS active: Complete this before March 31 deadlineThe March 31 deadline is almost here! Ensure your PPF, SSY, and NPS contributions are up-to-date to avoid penalties and keep your accounts running smoothly.

Why Japan holds the world's third-largest strategic oil reserveJapan, one of the world's largest oil importers, may release oil from national reserves as Middle East tensions threaten supply. Why is the country considering this move, and why does it rely so heavily on imported oil? Let's have a look.

From $70 to above $110: Tracking crude oil's sharp rise since FebruaryCrude oil, which was trading close to $70 per barrel in early February, has now climbed to around $105–$110, marking one of the fastest rallies in recent years.

Will Sensex, Nifty recover tomorrow? 3 things stock market investors should knowDalal Street closed deep in the red as Gulf tensions and rising oil prices spooked investors, raising the key question of whether markets will recover tomorrow or stay under pressure.

G7 considers emergency oil reserve release amid Gulf crisisG7 finance ministers are scheduled to hold a call with Fatih Birol, executive director of the International Energy Agency (IEA), to discuss the impact of the ongoing war involving Iran on global oil markets.

Can crude oil rally trigger inflation in India? FM Nirmala Sitharaman explainsReplying in writing in Parliament, Nirmala Sitharaman said domestic price levels are currently near the lower end of the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) tolerance band, which should help limit the impact of higher crude prices on inflation.

Sensex, Nifty sink to near 1-year lows as crude oil jumps on Iran warThe Nifty 50 index fell 1.73% to close at 24,028.05, its lowest closing level in 10 months. The market's volatility (VIX) also surged to a 21-month-high, showing that uncertainty among investors has increased.

Is the world ready for the 'unthinkable' amid West Asia conflict? IMF chief asksGeorgieva said shipping activity through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped by about 90%. The narrow waterway is one of the most important energy routes in the world.

entertainment.headline: Actor Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas introduces special person, clarifies engagement buzz Telugu actor Bellamkonda Sai Sreenivas publicly introduced Kavya Reddy by sharing pictures from a private family event. The Telugu actor clarified that the occasion was not an engagement and that formal celebrations will be announced later.

Aamir Khan changes earlier OTT stance, announces Sitaare Zameen Par on Sony LIV After initially speaking about avoiding an OTT release for Sitaare Zameen Par, Aamir Khan has now given in to the trend and announced the film's streaming platform.

Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani avoids Toxic clash, because 'no unnecessary competition' Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai has got a new release date after the makers of Toxic postponed their film to June 5, creating a clash with the romantic comedy. The makers said they believe filmmakers should support each other rather than "compete unnecessarily."

Gold tracksuit, high kicks: Zeenat Aman shares BTS story from The Great Gambler song Did you know that Zeenat Aman was never a confident dancer? The veteran actor made this revelation with a video from her 1979 film The Great Gambler.

Fact Check: Truth behind Ranbir-Sai Pallavi's viral wedding pic from Ramayan sets A photo featuring Ranbir Kapoor and Sai Pallavi is going viral, with people claiming that it was leaked from the sets of Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayan. Here's the truth behind the widely shared photo.

Suriya's Karuppu not releasing on April 10, director RJ Balaji to share update soon Director and actor RJ Balaji has clarified that the much-anticipated film Karuppu, starring Suriya, will not release on April 10. The filmmaker revealed that an official release date will be confirmed in ten days.

Thaai Kizhavi, Tamil film anchored by 63-year-old actor, mints Rs 50 crore globally Thaai Kizhavi, led by Radikaa Sarathkumar, surpassed Rs 50 crore in worldwide box office earnings. It marks a rare industry milestone for a film without a male star succeeding and providing a boost to the Tamil film industry.

XO, Kitty 3 trailer: Lana Condor's Lara Jean returns as Kitty navigates love, school Lana Condor reprises her role as Lara Jean in the upcoming third season of XO, Kitty, joining the cast as Kitty navigates new challenges and relationships.

Mannara Chopra breaks down over Anurag Dobhal's crash, sends message to his parents Mannara Chopra shared an emotional video after YouTuber Anurag Dobhal's shocking crash livestream left fans concerned. The Bigg Boss 17 fame also made a heartfelt appeal to his family while sharing an update on his condition.

Trisha shouldn't have attended wedding with Vijay: Director Partibhan doubles down Actor-director R Parthiban has posted an eight-minute video addressing the controversy surrounding his comments on Trisha at a recent award show. He denied any deliberate wrongdoing and commented on her public appearance with Vijay at a wedding reception.

Dhurandhar 2 advance booking crushes Stree 2 in premiere, juggernaut loading Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is rewriting paid preview history at the box office. It has raced past major Hindi films and set the stage for a gigantic opening weekend.

Cut, rename, comply: How censor board is shaking South cinema The censor board's growing interventions are disrupting South film releases. This trend raises serious concerns about creative freedom and the future of South cinema.

Shaka Laka Boom Boom actor Kinshuk Vaidya, wife Diiksha welcome first child Actor Kinshuk Vaidya, famous for his role in Shaka Laka Boom Boom, has announced the birth of his first child with wife Diiksha Nagpal. The couple welcomed a baby boy on March 8.

Heated Rivalry's Hudson Williams, Francois Arnaud slam racist, abusive fan comments Stars of the popular show Heated Rivalry stood united against online hate and discrimination. Hudson Williams and Francois Arnaud shared identical Instagram posts pushing back against abusive online comments.

Rihanna home gunfire case: 35-year-old woman charged with attempted murder After being arrested, a 35-year-old woman has been charged with attempted murder for allegedly firing gunshots at Rihanna's Los Angeles home. The singer was inside the mansion at the time, but no injuries were reported.

Boong director Lakshmipriya Devi broke down while editing the film. Here's why Boong director Lakshmipriya Devi shared that she would break down every time she saw the footage, which is why the post-production saw a delay. She also spoke about the film winning an award at BAFTA.

If Rahman composes for Ramayana, don't think there's communal bias: Salim Merchant Singer-composer Salim Merchant responded to AR Rahman's comments on bias in the Hindi film industry. He pointed out that Rahman's role as composer for the upcoming film Ramayana shows that there is no communal problem in Bollywood.

Vladimir review: Rachel Weisz anchors a campus drama on desire, ego and obsessionRelease Date:Thu Mar 05, 2026 Netflix series Vladimir is a dark campus drama that explores obsession, ageing and desire within the charged atmosphere of academia. Starring Leo Woodall and Rachel Weisz, the show blends psychological tension with satire and midlife anxieties.

Ranveer Singh teases 'wrath of god' in new Dhurandhar 2 poster. Seen yet? Ranveer Singh has released a new poster from the upcoming action thriller Dhurandhar 2, intensifying anticipation among fans as the film's worldwide release draws near.

general news.headline: LPG crisis: Iran war bites Mumbai's vada pav and Bengaluru's iconic dosa Live UpdatesThe ripple effect of the Iran conflict and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global energy transit route, has led to an LPG cooking gas crunch in India. It has severely impacted the hospitality sector in Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu and Karnataka.

Live: Chaos in Lok Sabha amid govt-Opposition faceoff on motion against SpeakerBudget Session Live Updates: The Lok Sabha has taken up an Opposition-backed resolution seeking the removal of Speaker Om Birla, with the discussion presently in progress. The government has rejected the Opposition's demand for a separate discussion on the Middle East crisis in Parliament, stating that Jaishankar has already briefed both Houses on the matter. The second half of Parliament's Budget Session began on a turbulent note on Monday as Opposition MPs raised slogans in the Rajya Sabha while External Affairs Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar briefed the House on the situation in the Middle East. However, some parliamentarians are of the opinion that External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar's statement is not enough, the government must discuss the West Asia crisis and have a table talk with the opposition.

CBSE clarifies viral 'Rickroll' QR code in Class 12 maths paper, says exam security intactCBSE has clarified that the Class 12 mathematics board exam question papers are genuine and their security remains uncompromised after concerns emerged over a QR code linking to a YouTube video of 'Never Gonna Give You Up' by Rick Astley, a prank widely known online as "rickrolling".

BCCI announces Rs 131 crore for T20 World Cup winners. How much more than 2024?T20 World Cup 2026: BCCI has announced Rs 131 crore for the T20 World Cup-winning side, surpassing the Rs 125 crore awarded in 2024, as India became the first team to defend the title.

India's submariners go deep. Their health data never surfacesHandcrafted ByOur Editors Sealed inside a steel tube at crushing depth, breathing manufactured air, eating off the same table where surgery will be performed if something goes wrong, India's submariners inhabit one of Earth's most hostile environments.

Not a litre of oil will be exported, warns Iran as Trump threatens Hormuz takeoverTrump has threatened to take over the Strait of Hormuz, a vital transit route for global oil, if Iran attacked tankers and continued to target American and Israeli assets in the Middle East.

Military said it's more fun to sink them: Trump on destroying vs capturing Iran shipsUS President Donald Trump on Monday claimed that the US Navy has sunk 46 "top-of-the-line" Iranian naval ships so far. He made this claim while addressing a gathering of congressional Republicans in Washington DC.

Delhi, Gurugram and THIS unexpected Indian city on Asia-Pacific top 50 pizza listThree Indian pizzerias have made it to the 2026 edition of the 50 Top Pizza Asia-Pacific list, putting India on the global map for some of the best pizzas in the world.

Watch: MS Dhoni spotted on Chennai flight, passengers erupt in cheersA flight to Chennai turned lively as passengers spotted former India cricket captain MS Dhoni onboard, leading to cheers and a flurry of activity captured in a now-viral video.

Live: Contingency plans ready to protect supplies, says Saudi Aramco CEOUS-Israel Attacks Iran Live Updates: The Middle East war entered its 11th day with a dramatic escalation over global oil supplies. Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned they would not allow "one litre of oil" to leave the region if US and Israeli strikes continue, raising fears of a wider energy shock. US President Donald Trump responded with a stark threat, saying Washington would hit Iran "twenty times harder" if it blocked tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, which carries about a fifth of the world's oil. Markets have been rattled as tankers remain stranded and producers halt pumping, while Iran's security establishment appears to be rallying behind new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei amid signs the conflict could stretch longer than expected. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest updates on the Middle East crisis.

Olympic award for India ex-captain for using volleyball as a gender levellerFormer India volleyball captain Abhijit Bhattacharya is the Global Winner of the Gender Equality, Diversity and Inclusion (GEDI) Champions Award 2025 given by the International Olympic Committee. Because of his efforts, thousands of boys and girls in rural Assam now play organised sport through the community-driven Brahmaputra Volleyball League.

No gas, no dosa? LPG crisis threatens Bengaluru's iconic Vidyarthi BhavanBengalurean or not, it's almost impossible to not know about the iconic eatery Vidyarthi Bhavan. But today, the establishment stands on the verge of a shutdown due to an LPG crisis.

Ministry of loneliness to solo weddings: Why Japan is becoming the land of the lonelyBullet trains, neon cities, and perfect order, Japan looks like the future. But behind the efficiency lies a quieter story: a nation where more people are living life alone.

What will Balen Shah as PM mean to Nepal's roti-beti ties with India?Rapper-turned-Kathmandu mayor Balendra Shah, who once blasted India, China and the US in a viral post, is now poised to become the Prime Minister of Nepal. While the routing of pro-Beijing Communist parties points to a setback for China, what would Balen at the helm in Kathmandu mean to India?

Family turns Lucknow train coach into 'reel studio', passenger calls helplineManmohan Verma, who was travelling from Bilaspur to Lucknow, shared a video from inside the train that showed members of the family dancing and filming reels inside the coach.
Why Gen Z can afford a sabbatical at 27 but millennials often can'tGen Z packs a backpack for a sabbatical. Millennials pack school tiffins before logging in.

India builds the world's BIM, but who is training the engineers?India's construction industry stands at an unusual crossroads. Every year, thousands of engineers and architects leave universities with degrees in hand, yet companies continue to search for professionals who can work on real projects from day one. The gap between education and skill has quietly become one of the sector's biggest challenges.

Did AI in war get 165 schoolgirls killed in Iran?The deaths of over 165 schoolgirls in Iran's Minab has sparked a debate over AI's deployment in warfare. AI can identify targets faster than humans, but can it truly be trusted in war? Even a small error can cost hundreds of civilian lives. We analysed the available facts to understand if AI played a role in the Iran school bombing.

sports.headline: Wish you were here: Rinku Singh's emotional note for father after T20 World Cup win T20 World Cup: Rinku Singh shared an emotional note for his father on social media after India won the global event on Sunday. Last month, his father passed away following a prolonged battle with stage-4 liver cancer.

Gujarat Titans rope in Matthew Hayden as batting coach for IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans have brought in Australia great Matthew Hayden as batting coach ahead of IPL 2026. The former opener will work with captain Shubman Gill and the Titans' powerful batting unit to sharpen their approach.

Neymar back in Brazil friendly squad: Big boost ahead of FIFA World Cup Neymar has been included in Brazil's preliminary squad for friendlies against France and Croatia. But with Carlo Ancelotti closely monitoring his fitness, the Santos star faces a race against time to secure a place.

USA's Nathan Martin wins LA Marathon by 0.01 seconds, dramatic finish goes viral Nathan Martin produced a stunning late surge to win the Los Angeles Marathon by 0.01 seconds. A viral video of rival Michael Kamau briefly running off course added drama to the race's closest finish.

The player debate was Sanju vs Ishan. The answer turned out to be both We spent months debating Sanju Samson or Ishan Kishan. Then, together in the T20 World Cup 2026 final, both of them put up a sensational performance against New Zealand. Seems like the team knew something we didn't.

Kuldeep Yadav set to tie the knot days after T20 World Cup victory Kuldeep Yadav will marry Vanshika on March 14 at a Mussoorie resort. The T20 World Cup winner postponed his wedding to help India claim their third title before tying the knot.

The teams had been stranded in India following widespread airspace closures triggered by the Israel–Iran conflict.

Stop conspiracy theories, accept India is a top side: Pakistan cricket greatsFormer Pakistan cricketers have urged their cricket community to stop conspiracy theories and accept India's white-ball dominance. India successfully defended their T20 World Cup crown, beating New Zealand in a one-sided final on Sunday, March 8.

India won a World Cup, but it doesn't feel the same anymore. Here's whyIndia's T20 World Cup win triggered an unexpected question online: why did it feel muted? The answer lies beyond cricket, in psychology, national confidence, and how success reshapes collective emotion.

By the end of IPL 2026, we will have blueprint for ODI World Cup 2027: GambhirIndia head coach Gautam Gambhir has set his eyes on the ODI World Cup 2027. Having won the T20 World Cup, Gambhir said that he will be working on the blueprint for the next ICC competition during the IPL months.

All the pain and setbacks have made me stronger: Sanju SamsonSpeaking after arriving in Thiruvananthapuram, Sanju Samson thanked his close circle and supporters for their prayers. Samson won the Player of the Tournament award after smashing three successive 80-plus scores, including an 89 in the final against New Zealand in Ahmedabad on Sunday.

Sanju Samson's my dear pondatti message melts hearts after T20 World Cup heroicsSanju Samson revealed the personal struggles behind his T20 World Cup 2026 heroics, crediting wife Charulatha's unwavering support as India's Player of the Tournament reflected on triumph, family and mental resilience.

No Suryakumar Yadav in ICC's T20 World Cup Team of the TournamentThe International Cricket Council has announced the T20 World Cup's Team of the Tournament. India captain Suryakumar Yadav is not named in the team which has 4 Indian players in the line-up.

After Pakistan horror run, Gary Kirsten replaces Jayasuriya as Sri Lanka coachAfter a tumultuous six-month stint with Pakistan in 2024, former South Africa opener Gary Kirsten has been appointed Sri Lanka's head coach on a two-year contract. He replaces Sanath Jayasuriya, who stepped down after the team's early exit from the 2026 T20 World Cup.

Stay humble, smash 300: Gambhir sets sights on bigger goals after T20 World Cup winT20 World Cup 2026: Gautam Gambhir said India still has bigger goals after the T20 World Cup win, adding the team wants to push the limits of T20 batting and hopes to breach the 300-run mark in the future.

Sanju Samson set to receive hero's welcome in Trivandrum after T20 World Cup triumphSanju Samson is set to receive a hero's welcome in Thiruvananthapuram after starring in India's T20 World Cup triumph, with the Kerala government planning a special reception for the Player of the Tournament.

PV Sindhu pulls out of Swiss Open days after returning from chaos-hit DubaiPV Sindhu has pulled out of the Swiss Open after returning from a stressful three-day ordeal in Dubai. The travel disruption had earlier forced the two-time Olympic medallist to miss the All England Championships.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.
